conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions. Let me start by reading the official guidance on Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Now I can help you assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible. 

I have one child - my daughter Alex, who is 5 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know about Alex, who is 5 years old.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few more questions.

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your relationship with Alex.

**Do you live with Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, she lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

**Is Alex currently in hospital, care, or any residential accommodation, or has she been in any of these situations for an extended period?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, she's not in hospital or care or anything like that. She's just at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me:
- You live in the UK (Manchester)
- Alex is 5 years old (under 16)
- You live with Alex
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for her
- Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation

Let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the necessary criteria:
- ✓ You live in the UK
- ✓ Alex is under 16 years old (she's 5)
- ✓ You're responsible for Alex as you live with her
- ✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for her
- ✓ There are no special circumstances affecting eligibility

You can now proceed to apply for Child Benefit. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 5 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. According to the eligibility rules: (1) Alex is under 16 years old (she is 5), which meets the age requirement. (2) The claimant lives in the UK. (3) The claimant is responsible for Alex as they live together. (4) No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex - only one person can claim per child. (5) Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation for an extended period. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 5-year-old daughter Alex. They meet all the standard Child Benefit eligibility criteria: they are responsible for a child under 16, they live with the child, no one else is claiming for the child, and there are no special circumstances (such as the child being in hospital or care) that would affect eligibility. The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. According to the eligibility rules: (1) Alex is under 16 years old (she is 5), which meets the age requirement. (2) The claimant lives in the UK. (3) The claimant is responsible for Alex as they live together. (4) No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex - only one person can claim per child. (5) Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation for an extended period. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 5-year-old daughter Alex. They meet all the standard Child Benefit eligibility criteria: they are responsible for a child under 16, they live with the child, no one else is claiming for the child, and there are no special circumstances (such as the child being in hospital or care) that would affect eligibility. The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
